message:
feat(fuzz): add fuzz_signal for sig-family surface (bd-dvr22 priority-2)

Adds crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_signal.rs — 14 ops
covering POSIX signal surface: sigemptyset, sigfillset, sigaddset,
sigdelset, sigismember, sigprocmask, pthread_sigmask, sigpending,
sigaction, sigaltstack, sigsuspend, raise, kill, killpg.

Oracles:
- Return-code contract (0 / -1 / doc non-neg only).
- Set self-consistency: sigaddset(N) -> sigismember(N)==1;
  sigdelset(N) -> sigismember(N)==0.
- Mask round-trip: sigprocmask(SIG_SETMASK, new, &old) followed by
  sigprocmask(SIG_SETMASK, old, null) must restore byte-for-byte.
- Syscall differential on sigpending against libc::syscall(
  SYS_rt_sigpending).

Safety:
- Delivery-triggering ops (raise, kill, killpg) restricted to
  {0, SIGWINCH, SIGURG} — probe-only or default-ignored, so delivery
  to the fuzz binary is a no-op.
- sigaction only installs SIG_DFL / SIG_IGN — never an attacker-
  chosen handler pointer. Always captures oldact and restores it at
  the end of the op.
- Invalid signal numbers (65 > max) exercised only on set-
  manipulation ops that must return -1 EINVAL.
- sigsuspend is gated by a sigpending check — only enters the
  blocking call when a safe signal is already pending, so the harness
  never hangs on libFuzzer's iteration clock.
- Process-wide SIGLOCK serializes iterations.

Validation: cargo build --bin fuzz_signal clean (2.0s); cargo clippy
--bin fuzz_signal --no-deps -- -D warnings clean after one doc-
wording fix.

Continues bd-dvr22 drain (fuzz_mmap was priority-1, fuzz_signal is
priority-2; remaining: socket, fortify, setjmp, dlfcn, pthread,
c11threads — last two need TSan campaigns).

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -65,6 +65,11 @@ name = "fuzz_mmap"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_mmap.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_signal"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_signal.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_resolver"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resolver.rs"
